arts.ttl: Team News: Bournemouth vs. Arsenal injury, suspension list, predicted XIs
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest injury and suspension news ahead of Saturday's Premier League clash between Bournemouth and Arsenal.

arts.ttl: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta leaves door open to managing England following Thomas Tuchel appointment
arts.sum: Arsenal head coach Mikel Arteta discusses the possibility of managing England in the future following the appointment of Thomas Tuchel earlier this week.

arts.ttl: Arteta has perfect Arsenal blueprint to solve injury crisis for Bournemouth
arts.sum: Given the injury issues in his squad, Arteta was asked for updates on a number of stars with a mixture of positive and negative news delivered. Arteta was also on hand to offer the latest on Martinelli, Odegaard and Havertz with it sounding far less likely that trio make the game in comparison to the other four.

arts.ttl: What really happened when Celtic boss Brendan Rodgers met Kieran Tierney as boss spills the beans on Arsenal star chat
arts.sum: The rumour mill has shifted up a gear after the duo were snapped together in London

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta reveals key area Arsenal are 'struggling' with injuries
arts.sum: MIkel Arteta has revealed full-backs Ben White, Jurrien Timber and Oleksandr Zinchenko are all close to returning to full fitness but admitted Arsenal are "struggling" for numbers ahead of their trip to Bournemouth on Saturday. Speaking in his press conference on Friday, Arteta revealed: "They [White, Timber and Zinchenko] are quite close the three of them.

arts.ttl: Report: Arsenal offered the opportunity to sign £70m striker to lead their attack
arts.sum: According to TBR Football, Paris Saint-Germain attacker Randal Kolo Muani has been offered to Arsenal. The French attacker is not the first choice at PSG and Luis Enrique has favoured Goncalo Ramos in his attack. arts.ttl: Saka, Havertz, Odegaard, White - Arsenal injury news and return dates for Bournemouth - football.london
arts.sum: The October international break has been far from kind to Arteta with Saka and Gabriel Martinelli picking up fresh knocks - while Havertz and Thomas Partey remained at London Colney because of minor issues . Mikel Arteta has shared fresh updates on nine Arsenal injuries for the upcoming Premier League match against Bournemouth.

arts.ttl: Why Neto can't play for Arsenal in the Premier League this weekend
arts.sum: The 35-year-old was recently unable to feature for Arsenal in the Carabao Cup when they hosted League One's Bolton Wanderers. That time, Neto was unable to feature because he was cup tied having already played for Bournemouth in the second round defeat to West Ham United.

arts.ttl: Every word Mikel Arteta said on Saka fitness, Odegaard Arsenal return and Tuchel appointment
arts.sum: He’s done a few things in the last few days when he came back from the national team after the second game. He’s one of the best coaches in the world in my opinion, the way his teams are set up.
